notes: P2: code-graph-scan.vitest.ts does not assert currentContentHash propagation into isFileStale.
P2: scan tests still do not pin currentContentHash propagation; crash-recovery freshness test still passes under old mtime-only semantics.

P1: nested tsconfig extends are still single-level only; inline type-only imports are mis-tagged as value imports; regex fallback still drops resolver metadata needed for alias/type/barrel resolution. P2: barrel traversal is cycle-safe but unbounded.

P1: status silently reports zero drift when the edge baseline is missing or malformed, and malformed metadata blocks automatic reseeding. P1: share_drift is emitted as absolute magnitude instead of signed current_share - baseline_share deltas.

P1: gold verifier matches symbols case-insensitively, so case-only identifier regressions still pass. P1: persisted per-query verification results omit query/source context required by the partial-failure contract. P2: unsupported v2 probe warnings are emitted once per query instead of once per battery.

P1: detect_changes only blocks on failed verification when ensure-ready already returns verificationGate, but the real action:none branch drops that field; the new test mocks an unreachable fresh+verificationGate state. P2: lastSelfHealAt timestamps skipped/failed selective-reindex attempts, not completed self-heals.

P1: code_graph_verify trusts caller rootDir and can inline-index outside the workspace when allowInlineIndex=true. P1: batteryPath is an unrestricted arbitrary JSON read surface and persisted absolute paths/results are echoed back by status. P1: tsconfig extends can read files outside rootDir/workspace, and alias targets still probe external paths via statSync.

P0: direct-test traceability is missing for REQ-002/003/004/005/006/007/008/009/012/015; only REQ-001/010/011/013/014 are directly covered. P2: implementation-summary limitations 1 and 5 explain the current state but do not name a concrete future-fix path.

P1: gold-query-verifier duplicates the code_graph_query response envelope via any-typed parsing and mock-only tests, so verifier/query contract drift is not compiler- or integration-guarded.
